name: Cloud Migration
description: Plan and execute workload migration between cloud providers or from on-premises to cloud with minimal downtime.

inputs.name: source_environment
inputs.description: Current infrastructure specification including compute, storage, networking, and dependencies.

inputs.name: target_cloud
inputs.description: Destination cloud provider, region, and target architecture.

inputs.name: migration_strategy
inputs.description: Preferred approach: lift-and-shift, re-platform, or re-architect with phasing plan.

inputs.name: downtime_tolerance
inputs.description: Maximum acceptable downtime window and data consistency requirements.

outputs.name: migration_plan
outputs.description: Phased migration roadmap with workload prioritization and dependency ordering.

outputs.name: target_infrastructure
outputs.description: IaC definitions for the target environment matching source capabilities.

outputs.name: data_migration_report
outputs.description: Data transfer logs, checksums, and consistency verification results.

outputs.name: cutover_runbook
outputs.description: Step-by-step cutover procedure with DNS switch, traffic routing, and rollback steps.

gates.rule: Confirm the task is workload migration, not greenfield deployment or application refactoring.
Require a validated migration plan and target infrastructure before initiating data transfer.
Verify data integrity checksums match after each transfer batch before proceeding.
Return migration plan, target IaC, data migration report, and cutover runbook with rollback procedure.

steps.description: Inventory source workloads, dependencies, data volumes, and performance baselines.
Map source capabilities to target cloud services and design the target infrastructure.
Deploy target environment using IaC with networking, compute, storage, and security configurations.
Set up data replication pipelines with consistency checks and incremental sync.
Run functional tests, performance benchmarks, and security scans against the target.
Switch DNS, route traffic to target, and verify end-to-end functionality within downtime window.
Decommission source resources after validation period and archive migration artifacts.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read source infrastructure inventory, IaC templates, and migration plans.

tools.name: filesystem.write
tools.purpose: Write target IaC, migration reports, and cutover runbooks.

tools.name: shell.exec
tools.purpose: Execute cloud CLI commands, data transfer tools, and validation scripts.

verification.method: Source environment inventory covers 100% of production workloads.
Checksums match for all transferred data sets.
All functional tests pass on the target environment.
Traffic routes to target with zero errors within the downtime window.

success_criteria: All workloads are migrated with data integrity verified.
Target environment passes functional and performance tests.
Cutover completes within the defined downtime tolerance.
Source environment is safely decommissioned after validation.

rollback.when: If cutover validation fails, revert DNS and traffic routing to source environment within the downtime window.
